THE MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION IS TAKEN OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE MOTION FOR MANDATORY AND DISCRETIONARY RELIEF. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CHRISTINA ORTEGA'S MOTION FOR DISCRETIONARY AND MANDATORY RELIEF PURSUANT TO CCP 473B FROM SUMMARY JUDGMENT FOR DEFENDANT CCSF AND SUMMARY JUDGMENT FOR DEFENDANT CBRE - THE MOTION FOR MANDATORY RELIEF IS DENIED AND THE MOTION FOR DISCRETIONARY RELIEF IS GRANTED. THE ORDERS GRANTING SUMMARY JUDGMENT IN FAVOR OF DEFENDANT CCSF AND CB RICHARD ELLIS, INC. THAT WERE ENTERED ON DECEMBER 28, 2015 ARE VACATED. THE MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT BROUGHT BY CCSF AND CB RICHARD ELLIS, INC. ARE RE-SCHEDULED FOR HEARING ON FEBRUARY 24, 2016. ALL PARTIES ARE REQUIRED TO LODGE COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR FILINGS NO LATER THAN FEBRUARY 10, 2016 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Defendant Cb Richard Ellis, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-24-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. RAYMOND GHERMEZIAN, ESQ. of Raymond Ghermezian, APLC, Tel No. 323-900-5800 appearing telephonically for the Plaintiff;
Atty. ROBERT TALTZ, ESQ. of Tyson & Mendes, Tel No. 415-464-4935, appearing for Defendant CB Richard Ellis, Inc.;
Atty. JOHN RANUCCI, ESQ. appearing for Defendant City and County of San Francisco.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant CB Richard Ellis, Inc.'s Motion For Summary Judgment is denied. Plaintiff is permitted to amend her complaint to substitute the word "stairs" for "sidewalk." This amendment conforms to proof and the defendants are not prejudiced by the amendment. There is a triable issue whether CCSF or CBRE was responsible for the stairs where the injury occurred. The lease between CCSF and Enterprise (attached to the Ricasa declaration) shows that CBRE is responsible for the "Common Use" areas and the stairs are in that area. The deposition testimony of Enterprise's regional operations manager (Mr. Rudd) states that CBRE was responsible for the stairs. (Rudd Depo., 24:15-16; 25:3-6 & 20-24). Plaintiff shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for the Court's signature in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-24-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. RAYMOND GHERMEZIAN, ESQ. of Raymond Ghermezian, APLC, Tel No. 323-900-5800 appearing telephonically for the Plaintiff;
Atty. ROBERT TALTZ, ESQ. of Tyson & Mendes, Tel No. 415-464-4935, appearing for Defendant CB Richard Ellis, Inc.;
Atty. JOHN RANUCCI, ESQ. appearing for Defendant City and County of San Francisco.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant City And County Of San Francisco's Motion For Summary Judgment is denied. Plaintiff is permitted to amend her complaint to substitute the word "stairs" for "sidewalk." This amendment conforms to proof and the defendants are not prejudiced by the amendment. There is a triable issue whether the wet and worn stairs constituted a dangerous condition of public property rather than a trivial defect. There is also a triable issue whether CCSF had constructive notice of the defect. The declaration of plaintiff's expert (Mr. Avrit) and exhibits 3 and 4 to plaintiff's deposition show that the wet stairs were worn and such wear was a substantial factor in causing plaintiff to fall. A trier of fact could reasonably infer that the wear occurred over a substantial period of time. There is also a triable issue whether a reasonable inspection would have disclosed the assertedly dangerous condition. (Stanford v. City of Ontario (1972) 6 Cal.3d 870, 884.) The objections to the Avrit declaration are overruled. Plaintiff shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for the Court's signature in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Discretionary And Mandatory Relief Pursuant To Ccp 473b From Summary Judgment For Defendant Ccsf And Summary Judgment For Defendant Cbre
Off Calendar Feb-09-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. RAYMOND GHERMEZIAN, ESQ. of Raymond Ghermezian, APLC, Tel No. 323-900-5800 appearing telephonically for the Plaintiff;
Atty. ROBERT TAITZ, ESQ. of Tyson & Mendes, Tel No. 415-464-4935 appearing for Defendant CB Richard Ellis;
Atty. JOHN RANUCCI, ESQ. of Lombardi, Loper & Conant, LLP, Tel No. 510-610-4766 appearing for Defendant City and County of San Francisco.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFF CHRISTINA ORTEGA'S Motion For Discretionary And Mandatory Relief Pursuant To CCP 473b From Summary Judgment For Defendant CCSF And Summary Judgment For Defendant CBRE, Judge Goldsmith is unavailable to hear this motion because he is retired. The motion for mandatory relief is denied and the motion for discretionary relief is granted. Mandatory relief is unavailable because the mandatory relief provision of CCP 473 does not apply in these circumstances. (Prieto v. Loyola Marymount University (2005) 132 Cal.App.4th 290, 295.) Discretionary relief per CCP 473 is granted because the declarations of Mr. Lopez, Mr. Mortarotti, and Mr. Ghermezian demonstrate mistake, inadvertence, and/or excusable neglect and granting such relief is consistent with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits. Thee orders granting summary judgment in favor of defendant CCSF and CB Richard Ellis, Inc. that were entered on December 28, 2015 are vacated. The motions for summary judgment brought by CCSF and CB Richard Ellis, Inc. are re-scheduled for hearing on February 24, 2016. All parties are required to lodge courtesy copies of their filings no later than February 10, 2016 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
